observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Disposal Committee to conduct immediate disposal of all unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular 92-386 to prevent further deterioration and loss of additional income.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in
identifying the details of the reconciling items identified in the BRS and effect
the necessary adjustments; and

b. Municipal Treasurer to be proactive in making the necessary representation
with source agencies and bank regarding the details of the funds credited to the
bank account and identified as reconciling items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the issuance of
the semi-expendable items to the end-users based on the RIS or any equivalent
document.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to expedite the documentation pertinent to the applicable condition
which caused the dormancy of the receivable accounts and unliquidated fund
transfers to facilitate the submission of the request for write-off or derecognition
thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee, through the Property and
Supply Management Officer, to conduct the necessary reconciliation of the
variances between the accounting records and RPCI for the four inventory
accounts to serve as basis for making the necessary adjustments; and

b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect
the correct balance of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the increase in the
appropriation for travel of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and direct the
Municipal Treasurer to:

a. deposit intact the full amount of collections in the AGDB at least once a
week, or as soon as the collections reach P10,000.00;

b. carefully check the total collections per official receipts (ORs)/community tax
certificates (CTCs) and the amounts recorded in RCDs;

c. submit the original copy of OR No. 7280308 that was allegedly cancelled or
execute affidavit of loss and request for relief if the same cannot be located and
submitted to the Audit Team;

d. ensure that (i) all CTCs and ORs are attached to the RCD; (ii) the details such
as date, name, and/or signature of the Collecting Officer/AO are
affixed/indicated in the ORs; (iii) all modifications in the OR are
countersigned; (iv) the right thumb print, name and signature of the taxpayer
are affixed in the CTCs; (v) COA’s copy of ORs/CTC is an exact carbon copy
of the original; (vi) inclusive serial numbers and quantity of ORs/CTCs are
correctly recorded in the RCDs and Report of Accountability for Report of
Accountability Forms (RAAFs); and (vi) all sections of the RCDs are properly
filled out; and

e. ensure that the AO who will deposit the collections is duly designated and is
an adequately bonded AO of the Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry for the misstatement noted in the
recorded collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Disposal
Committee to conduct immediate disposal of all unserviceable properties
following the procedures set forth in COA Circular 92-386 to prevent further
deterioration and loss of additional income.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. LDRRMO to ensure that the disaster risk-related PPAs are (i) timely
implemented for benefit of the constituents of the Municipality; and (ii)
properly classified as MF or QRF activities; and

b. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entry to
reclassify the completed projects that remained in the CIP – Infra Asset
account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Local Finance Committee to cause the immediate submission of the
revised Annual Investment Plan (AIP) which conforms with the Annual
Operating Budget (AOB) or submit satisfactory explanation in the absence
thereof;

b. Municipal Engineer to: (i) ensure that the plans and programs are properly
accomplished and prepared in such a way that it can be readily implemented;
and (ii) ensure that projects are implemented within the target duration; and

c. Municipal Accountant see to it that (i) all the necessary documentary
requirements attached to the vouchers are accurate and complete before
processing the claims; and (ii) effect the necessary correcting entries for the
inconsistent recording of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts as at
year end for proper presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to (i)
explain the above-noted lapses/deficiencies; (ii) use the standard forms provided
in the GPPB Circular No. 04-2020 for contract agreement and bid form; (iii) post
the NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPS website, within three and 15 calendar days
from its issuance, respectively; (iv) indicate the date of receipt of the Performance
Bonds/Securities and ensure that the validity is in accordance with Sections
39.3 and 39.4 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184; (v) furnish the Audit
Team with a copy of the Contract Agreement and its attachments within five
working days from the execution thereof; (vi) submit the identified lacking
documents; (vii) conduct thorough bid evaluation and post-qualification
procedures to avoid overlapping personnel and equipment for simultaneously
implemented projects and carefully check the documents submitted by the
bidders to avoid discrepancies; and (viii) provide manpower support to the BAC
Secretariat and establish a system of accountability on the part of the BAC to
ensure that the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 will be
observed in all stages of the procurement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Engineer to (i) submit satisfactory explanation on the
observations noted; and (ii) carefully check and evaluate the correctness of
information stated in the documents submitted by the contractors; and

b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents are
accurate before payments are made to the contractors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to (i)
explain the above-noted deficiencies; (ii) submit the identified lacking documents
and the copy of CAFOA signed by the Municipal Accountant; and (iii) provide
manpower support to the BAC Secretariat and establish a system of
accountability on the part of the BAC to ensure that the provisions of the 2016
Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 will be observed in all stages of the procurement
process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to ensure that the fidelity bond of all AOs is effective or promptly
renewed 30 days prior to its expiration, before performing duties and
responsibilities relative to the handling of collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee, through the Property and Supply Management Officer, to ascertain and analyze the breakdown/composition of the unaccounted discrepancy of Other Supplies and Materials for Distribution and Fuel, Oil, and Lubricants Inventory accounts amounting to P73,043.52 between the accounting records and RPCI and reconcile their records as basis for making the necessary adjustments therein; and b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the balances of Office Supplies and Other Supplies and Materials Inventory accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
The Municipal Accountant conducted a detailed analysis of the discrepancy and drew Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 202401169 dated 01/24/2024 to correct the noted discrepancy.
The Municipal Accountant has drawn JEV No. 202401169 dated 01/24/2024 to correct the error affecting the inventory account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Treasurer to practice the sending of written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts; and b. Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare necessary adjusting entries for the staled checks; (ii) submit the identified bank statement, and schedule of outstanding checks to the Audit Team for evaluation; and (iii) submit the correct BRS under the General Fund – Port account.
observations.managementAction:
(i) The Municipal Treasurer practiced the sending of written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale to prevent a check from becoming stale. The Municipal Treasurer also coordinated with the Municipal Accountant to monitor and identify stale checks for adjustment in the accounting books.
(ii) The Municipal Accountant has already prepared the adjusting entries. (ii) The Municipal Accountant submitted the identified BRS, bank statements, and schedule of outstanding checks to the Audit Team for evaluation; (iii) submitted the correct BRS under the General Fund – Port account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. MDRRMO to (i) properly classify DRRM-related PPAs as to whether they are MF or QRF and cause the timely implementation thereof; (ii) coordinate with the Accounting Office when preparing the LDRRMFIP to ensure that the correct balance of the STF is incorporated therein; and (iii) as the Requisitioning Unit of the procured medicines, submit satisfactory explanation on the absence of certification that the medicines conform with the PNF, Volume I, 8th Edition; b. Property and Supply Management Officer to prepare the inventory list of all procured and donated DRRM items based on the updated stock and property cards; and c. Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers including transactions which involve the procurement of medicines; and (ii) transfer the accurate amount of unspent DRRM funds from the General Fund to the Trust Fund.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the posting of the reports on their appropriation, receipt and utilization of LDRRMF and other funds in the official website of the Municipality in adherence with the transparency principle of good governance.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant transferred the correct amount of unspent DRRM funds from GF to TF in January 2024 per RCD No. 2401007.
The MDRRMO submitted an inventory list of all the procured donated DRRM items to the Property and Supply Officer.
The Municipal Accountant implemented transfer of unspent DRRM funds and the Municipal Government created their official website www.bantonromblon.gov.v.ph. The LDRRM-related reports were posted on the website.
The Municipal Accountant submitted certifications that the drugs and medicines procured fall within and conform with the PNDF Volume 1.
observations.reason: Some activities related to DRRM Response were still classified as mitigating activities due to the erroneous classification by the LDRRMO. Reiterated in Observation No.7, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee, through the Property and Supply Management Officer, to conduct the necessary reconciliation of the variances between the accounting records and RPCI for the four inventory accounts to serve as basis for making the necessary adjustments; and b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team during the exit conference that she has drawn a journal entry on March 21, 2025 to correct the errors noted by the Audit Team. A copy of the JV shall be submitted for the Team’s validation.
The Municipal Accountant has drawn a journal entry on March 21, 2025 to correct the errors noted.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry for the misstatement noted in the recorded collections.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer, through letter dated March 27, 2025, submitted a copy of JV No. 2025-03-530 of even date, effecting the accounting entries prepared by the Municipal Accountant, to correct the errors noted. She committed that the recommendations pertaining to the other lapses will be implemented. The succeeding RCDs will be prepared judiciously to avoid the recurrence of the observations noted. In addition, she informed the Audit Team that her office already exerted earnest efforts to locate the original copy of OR No. 7280308 that was allegedly cancelled but to no avail.
The Municipal Accountant, through letter dated March 28, 2025, submitted a copy of JV No. 100-2024-04-721 dated April 22, 2024.
observations.reason: With regard to the original copy of OR No. 7280308 that could not be located, the Municipal Accountant should execute an Affidavit of Loss and formally notify the Audit Team. For the adjusting entries, all errors were adjusted except entries related to JEV Nos. 202401185 and 2024122071 under the General Fund and 202412137 under SEF; the Municipal Treasurer insisted amounts were correct but Audit Team found errors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. LDRRMO to ensure that the disaster risk-related PPAs are (i) timely implemented for benefit of the constituents of the Municipality; and (ii) properly classified as MF or QRF activities; and b. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entry to reclassify the completed projects that remained in the CIP – Infra Asset account.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Budget Officer (former OIC-MPDC) explained that the PPA for the damage assessment and validation of affected household per barangay was intended to be conducted immediately after the calamity and would have served as basis for the declaration of a state of calamity.
The Municipal Accountant, through letter dated March 28, 2025, submitted a copy of JV No. 300-202502-035 dated February 21, 2025 under the Trust Fund to transfer the completed project in its proper PPE account. She promised to submit the JV under the General Fund. Management during exit conference expressed commitment to timely implement DRRM-related PPAs.
observations.reason: The Audit Team iterated that mitigating activities should not be dependent on the occurrence of a calamity. If intended as mitigating, the PPA should be implemented regardless of occurrence; otherwise reprogramming before year-end is needed. Some transfers implemented (JV under Trust Fund) but JV under General Fund pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Local Finance Committee to cause the immediate submission of the revised Annual Investment Plan (AIP) which conforms with the Annual Operating Budget (AOB) or submit satisfactory explanation in the absence thereof; b. Municipal Engineer to: (i) ensure that the plans and programs are properly accomplished and prepared in such a way that it can be readily implemented; and (ii) ensure that projects are implemented within the target duration; and c. Municipal Accountant see to it that (i) all the necessary documentary requirements attached to the vouchers are accurate and complete before processing the claims; and (ii) effect the necessary correcting entries for the inconsistent recording of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts as at year end for proper presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer explained that the observation is attributable to adjustments effected to the Municipality’s NTA after the preparation of the AIP; while AOB was adjusted, the AIP was not. He promised revisions to the AIP. The Municipal Engineer explained lapses were due to shortage of personnel in MEO; they are now undertaking drafting of plans. The Municipal Accountant, through letter dated March 28, 2025, submitted a copy of JV No. 100-202503-533 dated March 21, 2025 to rectify the accounting error noted.
observations.reason: Some revised AIP submission and corrective entries made; however, some documentary deficiencies and inconsistent accounting entries were attributed to omission due to voluminous transactions and manpower constraints; further review and corrections ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to (i) explain the above-noted lapses/deficiencies; (ii) use the standard forms provided in the GPPB Circular No. 04-2020 for contract agreement and bid form; (iii) post the NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPS website, within three and 15 calendar days from its issuance, respectively; (iv) indicate the date of receipt of the Performance Bonds/Securities and ensure that the validity is in accordance with Sections 39.3 and 39.4 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184; (v) furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Contract Agreement and its attachments within five working days from the execution thereof; (vi) submit the identified lacking documents; (vii) conduct thorough bid evaluation and post-qualification procedures to avoid overlapping personnel and equipment for simultaneously implemented projects and carefully check the documents submitted by the bidders to avoid discrepancies; and (viii) provide manpower support to the BAC Secretariat and establish a system of accountability on the part of the BAC to ensure that the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 will be observed in all stages of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Head of the BAC Secretariat, through letter dated April 8, 2025, informed the Audit Team that they have already implemented corrective measures such as procedural review, use of required forms to prevent the recurrence of similar issues in the future, conduct of audit of past documents, improvement of knowledge on procurement, implementation of more robust tracking system for required documentation, and compliance with updated COA Circulars and GPPB Resolutions. They will review their evaluation process to ensure that it is in full compliance with existing policies. They shall ensure that all evaluations are thoroughly documented and the criteria therefor are clearly communicated to all stakeholders involved. They committed to take necessary steps to address the audit findings; scheduled a meeting to review recommendations and formulate an action plan; committed to procedural review and additional manpower support.
observations.reason: Deficient and lacking documentation, erroneous computations, and overlapping personnel and equipment indicative of incomplete bid evaluation and post-qualification procedures; attributable to inadvertence and manpower constraint. Some corrective actions planned/initiated; full compliance pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Engineer to (i) submit satisfactory explanation on the observations noted; and (ii) carefully check and evaluate the correctness of information stated in the documents submitted by the contractors; and b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents are accurate before payments are made to the contractors.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer, through letter dated April 11, 2025, signified his commitment to prevent the recurrence of the above observation by conducting regular inspection on all their projects and making their own photo documentation to make sure that all works were according to plans and specifications. They will also be stringent on scrutinizing submitted site photos versus actual accomplishments especially on straight contract projects to ensure that only accomplished works will be paid by the Municipality.
The Municipal Accountant committed to ensure that supporting documents are accurate before payments are made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to (i) explain the above-noted deficiencies; (ii) submit the identified lacking documents and the copy of CAFOA signed by the Municipal Accountant; and (iii) provide manpower support to the BAC Secretariat and establish a system of accountability on the part of the BAC to ensure that the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 will be observed in all stages of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, through letter dated March 28, 2025, submitted a copy of CAFOA No. 1139-12-24-100 duly signed by her. Management committed to check judiciously the documents relative to the procurement of goods to achieve appropriate and complete documentation of the procurement activities. They also noted the issue on the need of additional manpower support to the Office of the BAC.
observations.reason: Deficient and lacking documentation and erroneous computations indicative that the BAC has not conducted a thorough bid evaluation and post-qualification procedures; attributable to inadvertence and manpower constraint; validity of POs/payments remains doubtful pending submission of lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the fidelity bond of all AOs is effective or promptly renewed 30 days prior to its expiration, before performing duties and responsibilities relative to the handling of collections.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer commented during the exit conference that she already applied the renewal of bond before its expiration but during that time, there were errors noted by the Bureau of Treasury that needed revisions. However, delay was incurred on the submission of the revised forms due to the absence of the signatories concerned.
The Municipal Treasurer expressed her commitment to implement the audit recommendation by allocating sufficient time allowance for the renewal of bond to avoid gap period on the accountability of the AOs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to: (i) prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the format prescribed in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in order to ensure proper and timely monitoring and implementation thereof and also to avoid the need of securing a separate approval from the MDRRMC every time PPA/s are identified to be charged against said fund.
observations.managementAction: Coordination between the offices of the LDRRM and Accounting in the preparation of LDRRMFIP was made. The MDRRMO prepared the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the format prescribed and included the list of projects and activities. Posting of reports on their official website was done: www.bantonromblon.gov.v.ph and LDRRM-related reports were posted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. MPDC to ensure that a Supplemental Investment Program will be prepared and submitted for any reprogrammed PPA; b. Local Finance Committee to be diligent in the review of PPAs and to discourage approval of PPAs that are expressed in generic terms. Otherwise, proper authority from the sanggunian concerned should be secured; c. Municipal Engineer to: (i) ensure that the plans and programs are properly accomplished and prepared in such a way that it can be readily implemented; and (ii) ensure that projects are implemented within the target duration; and d. Municipal Accountant see to it that (i) all the necessary documentary requirements attached to the vouchers are accurate and complete before processing the claims; and (ii) effect the necessary correcting entries for the erroneous recording of PPE accounts and payable accounts as at year end for proper presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Implemented actions: Municipal Development Council (MDC) and Sangguniang Bayan (SB) resolutions were attached to the Supplemental Investment Program for reprogrammed PPAs for CY 2024. Disapproval of PPAs expressed in generic terms has been practiced. The Municipal Engineer submitted ABCs and plans for the five projects. The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary adjusting journal entry to correct erroneous accounting entries. Some lacking documents still noted in disbursement claims due to oversight; Municipal Engineer is undertaking drafting of plans to avoid future issues.
observations.reason: Lacking documents were still noted in some disbursement vouchers because these were overlooked by the Municipal Accountant due to voluminous transactions. Some implementation issues attributed to shortage of personnel in the Municipal Engineering Office.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (i) direct the concerned contractors to correct and rectify the deficiencies and invoke Sections 62.2.1 and 62.2.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (ii) for projects implemented through “By Administration”, require the Municipal Engineer to institute corrective actions on the defects noted to avoid further damage and wastage of government fund; and (iii) thru the Municipal Engineer submit to the Audit Team compliance report together with the sufficient pictures of the rectification of deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: Deficiencies of the six completed infrastructure projects were already rectified. For projects implemented through 'By Administration', defects noted have been rectified by the management. Pictures of rectifications were submitted by the Municipal Engineering Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the Municipal Engineer, as the head of Implementing Unit and Inspectorate Committee accomplish and submit the necessary documents mentioned above to support the implementation of the PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: Management submitted the supporting documents for the projects noted in CY 2020 Audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed road networks property cards, property ledger cards and subsidiary records for the account and disclose the information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the provision cited under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: Road Network Property Cards were already submitted to the Audit Team on September 30, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned municipal official and employee to exert extra efforts to collect from the recipients of the KKK and SEA livelihood loan programs. If it can no longer be collected, the Municipal Accountant expedite the documentation for the request for write-off of said dormant receivable account. Thereafter, request the authority to write-off account pursuant with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: Collection letters were already sent to recipients and/or family in case of death of the recipients.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to (a) coordinate in identifying the details of the book reconciling items indicated in the BRS and effect the necessary adjustments; and (b) observe the procedural guidelines on the cancellation/replacement of stale checks and prepare the journal entries in the books for fair presentation of balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) communicate with the LBP-Romblon and refer the problems encountered with the online remittance; and (b) direct the Municipal Treasurer to complete and/or finish the transfer of its funds from Philippine National Bank to Authorized Government Depository Bank (e.g. Land Bank of the Philippines and Development Bank of the Philippines), pursuant to Department of Finance Circular No. 001-2015 and DOF Department Order No. 27-05 to ensure judicious management in a manner supportive of the development objectives of the government in accelerating economic growth and stability of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the (i) Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry to effect the necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recording; and (ii) enjoin the Supply Officer and LDRRMO to comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 on the preparation of required monthly reports.

We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate personnel to create and maintain an official website of the Municipality wherein reports on appropriation, receipts and utilization of LDRRMF and other funds will be posted in adherence with the transparency governing principle of good governance pursuant to Section 3(f), Rule 1 of IRR of R.A. No. 10121 also known as the PDRRM Act of 2010.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator:
submit reasons/explanations for the delayed and non-implementation of the aforementioned projects to enable the Audit Team to recommend a doable measure to facilitate implementation thereof; and
properly evaluate, determine the expected output and properly plan the schedule of implementation of the unimplemented development PPAs, in line with its Local Development Plan.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor regularly monitor its implementation, in accordance with the approved AIP of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Government thru the MPDC (a) render satisfactory explanation on the noted improper appropriation of PPAs under the 20 percent DF; and (b) strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in identifying the PPAs to be funded by the 20 percent Development Fund and ensure that they partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) render satisfactory explanation on the noted improper charging to the 20 percent DF; and (b) henceforth, ensure that expenditures which partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes are the only ones charged to the fund.

We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (i) direct the concerned contractors to correct and rectify the deficiencies and invoke Sections 62.2.1 and 62.2.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (ii) for projects implemented through “By Administration”, require the Municipal Engineer to institute corrective actions on the defects noted to avoid further damage and wastage of government fund; and (iii) thru the Municipal Engineer submit to the Audit Team compliance report together with the sufficient pictures of the rectification of deficiencies.

We also reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Engineer ensure that all projects have billboards/signboards posted at the vicinity of the project location in compliance with Section 2.2 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013. Evidence of installation of billboards/signboards on the project site should likewise be attached to the claims for payment of the corresponding PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with the reporting requirements under COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 and ensure that all government PPAs are included in the list before its submission to the Audit Team to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Government thru the Environment and Natural Resources Office avoid burning of waste materials in an open area and observe properly the segregation thereof in compliance with pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9003 and for the full protection of the environment and general welfare of the constituents residing within the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer together with the Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of Inventories, reconcile the results thereof with accounting records and submit the same to the Audit Team.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Treasurer, reconcile their records with respect to PPE accounts pursuant to Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs in order for the audit team to ascertain the existence, validity and correctness of Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment as well as the reliability of balances presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties pursuant to Sections 163 and 167 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management for Local Government and Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and follow the procedures cited in Sections 379 to 381 of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 89-296, to avoid further asset value deterioration and overstatement of PPE balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to complete and/or finish the transfer of its funds from Philippine National Bank (PNB) to Authorized Government Depository Bank (e.g. Land Bank of the Philippines and Development Bank of the Philippines), pursuant to Sections 5.2, 5.4 and 5.5 of the Department of Finance (DOF) Circular No. 001-2015 dated June 1, 2015 and Paragraph 1 of DOF Department Order No. 27-05 dated December 9, 2005, to ensure judicious management in a manner supportive of the development objectives of the government in accelerating economic growth and stability of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRMO to incorporate the unimplemented PPAs in the succeeding year’s LDRRMFIP and ensure proper implementation thereof in accordance with its approved planned schedule of implementation, pursuant to Section 1, Rule 18 of the IRR of RA 10121.

We recommended that the LDRRMO ensure the monthly submission of LDRRM Fund utilization report for the succeeding period to help the management in proper monitoring of its utilization and the Audit Team in timely verification of the correctness of expenses charged therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and LDRRMO maintain close coordination for the effective, efficient and reconciled preparation and presentation of the reported balances reflected on their respective reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Local Finance Committee (LFC):
Prepare monthly report on fund utilization and status of Program/Project/Activity Implementation funded from the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities (BGCM); and
Post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous places in the locality at the end of every month.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor send a written notice when said reports have been posted in its website to the DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance, and other offices where the submission of reports is required under existing laws, rules and regulations, pursuant to Section 4.4 of the DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 07, 2020.

We therefore recommended that the Municipality thru the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) ensure the preparation of Annual Procurement Plan in the succeeding year as well as it’s optimum utilization by spearheading the efficient and effective implementation of the appropriated fund which by its purpose should have mitigate if not contain the spread of COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator properly evaluate, determine the expected output and properly plan the schedule of implementation of the unimplemented development PPAs, in line with its Local Development Plan.  We further recommended that Local Chief Executive regularly monitor their implementation, in accordance with the approved AIP of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Government thru the MPDC strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in identifying the programs/projects/activities to be funded by the 20% Development Fund and ensure that they partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the Provincial Engineer, as the head of Implementing Unit and Inspectorate Committee accomplish and submit the necessary documents mentioned above to support the implementation of the PPAs.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure completeness and propriety of the supporting documents for all infrastructure projects before making payments thereof and recording to the corresponding accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the proper use of accounts in recording transactions, review the existing journal entries and effect the necessary correcting entries for proper presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and/or the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the reporting and publicity requirements under COA Circular No. 2013-004 and ensure that all government PPAs are included in the list before its submission to the Audit Team to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation thereof.
We also recommended that the Provincial Engineer be guided by Section 2.2 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 and ensure that all projects have tarpaulins/signboards posted at the vicinity of the project location. Evidence of installation of tarpaulins/signboards on the project site should likewise be attached to the claims for payment of the corresponding PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; (b) Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year to the Audit Team; and (c) Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed road networks property cards, property ledger cards and subsidiary records for  the account and disclose the information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the provision cited under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance to Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs to allow the audit team to ascertain the existence, validity and correctness of Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment as well as the reliability of balances presented in the financial statements.







	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer together with the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of the properties, reconcile the results thereof with accounting records and submit the same to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs to allow the audit team to ascertain the existence, validity and correctness of Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment as well as the reliability of balances presented in the financial statements.

We likewise recommended that Municipal Accountant provide depreciation for the PPE accounts totaling P1,291,262.59 supported with a lapsing schedule to ensure the accuracy of the amount provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the municipal officials concerned to develop and submit a gender-responsive GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) which is reviewed and duly approved by the DILG Regional Office and incorporated to the agency’s Annual Budget, and to observe compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the GAD Focal person to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: It was observed that the municipality again failed to submit reports required relative to GAD for CY 2017 thus AOM No. 2018-016 dated Feb. 20, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance to Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs that the Report on the Physical Count on Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPR) be submitted after the conduct of the physical count to allow the audit team to ascertain the reliability and accuracy of the balances presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and record adjustments for valid reconciling items. Moreover, adopt appropriate measures on the prompt receipt of bank statements in order that bank reconciliation statements are properly prepared and timely submitted for the verification of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize every disbursement, determine the nature of the transaction, examine the attached supporting documents and properly classify and record the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Budget Officer observe strictly the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 6.3.6 of LBC No. 98.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the fundamental principle provided in Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 that claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation and be guided with Section 40 (2) Book IV of E.O. No. 292 and Section 04 (s) of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify balances of Accounts Payable outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claims, administrative or judicial, have been filed or are not covered with perfected contracts on record and those found without valid claim or undocumented, irrespective of age, and cause their reversion to the unappropriated surplus for fair presentation of the account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned municipal official and employee to exert extra efforts to collect from the recipients of the KKK and SEA livelihood loan programs. If it can no longer be collected, the Municipal Accountant expedites the documentation for the request for write-off of said dormant receivable account. Thereafter, request the authority to write-off account pursuant with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all payments of allowances and other benefits to municipal officials and employees, shall be based on and conform strictly with laws, rules and regulations granting or authorizing such payments. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant scrutinize supporting documents supporting the claim. Cause the refund of the unauthorized and excess payment of hazard and subsistence allowances totaling ₱112,817.00 received by the concerned employees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly implement the rules and regulations as provided by LBC No. 102 in the grant of RATA and instruct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize supporting documents to said allowances. Cause the refund of the overpayment of RATA received by the concerned municipal officials and employees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collections as mandated in the afore-cited provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and NGAS Manual Volume I. Moreover, the Municipal Mayor, thru the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, should strengthen the internal controls in the management of collections and deposits.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant, strengthen the internal control in the withholding and remittance of GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth contributions, loan amortizations and premiums payments, respectively. Moreover, investigate and examine the year-end balances of the said account, and draw entries for adjustments, if necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant, strengthen the internal control in the withholding and remittance of taxes to ensure that required taxes are withheld and correct amount of tax due are remitted.

We recommended that the management should strictly follow Section 47 of the General Provisions of R.A. No. 10924 by screening the loan applications of its officials and employees so that their monthly net take home pay will not be reduced to an amount lower than Four Thousand Pesos (₱4,000.00), after all authorized deductions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all concerned officials and employees be guided that financial transactions and operations of any government agency shall be governed of the fundamental principle that claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor implement the allocation of 1% of Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of their Municipal Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC) in accordance with Section 15 of R.A. No. 9344 and DILG Memo. Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to observe strict compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 specifically in the use of the prescribed format of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) and the preparation and submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to strictly comply the reporting and publicity requirements for government programs, projects and activities provided under COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to promote public awareness in the use of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the concerned municipal officials and employees concerned observe strict compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and JMC No. 2016-01 in developing a gender-responsive Gender and Development Plan and Budget (GPB) in gender mainstreaming and the localization of Magna Carta for Women that will be incorporated to agency’s annual budget.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collections as mandated in the afore-cited provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and NGAS Manual Volume I.

observations.reason: It was observed that collections were not deposited promptly for CY 2017 hence issuance of AOM No. 2018-009 dated Feb. 13, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that: Reconciling items are properly investigated/documented and immediately effected in the books of accounts to include credit and debit memoranda, stale checks, etc. and to see to it that the Municipal Accountant draws journal vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the General Ledger so that book and bank balances are always reconciled at any given period; and The Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) are properly prepared and timely submitted for the verification of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Accountant for timely reconciliation of accounts to include credit and debit memoranda, stale checks, etc., and to draw journal vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the General Ledger; and to ensure that Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) are properly prepared and timely submitted for the verification of accounts.
observations.reason: It was observed that bank reconciliation statements for some months of CY 2017 was not prepared and submitted due to the late receipt of bank statements from depository banks. AOM No. 2018-019 dated Feb. 22, 2018 was issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Budget Officer observe strictly the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 6.3.6 of LBC No. 98. Moreover, based on local circumstances and priorities, the following measures may be adopted to address the observed condition: Intensify tax collection efficiency and invest in revenue-generating projects or activities to increase the income; Hiring of additional personnel shall be kept at the minimum especially if their services are not crucial to the agency; Strong support for a continuing tight control of expenditures related to personal services.
observations.managementAction: Intensified Tax Collection and No additional hiring of personnel.
observations.reason: The municipality failed to observe strictly the general limitations on personnel services thus, AOM No. 2018-17 dated Feb. 21, 2018 was issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to remit the excess and unutilized PDAF or CDF to the Bureau of Treasury/DOF and ask Official Receipt for the same in compliance to Sections 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and pursuant to Supreme Court pronouncement declaring PDAF unconstitutional.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to account all unutilized PDF/CDF and remit the same to the Bureau of the Treasury and remit the same to the Bureau of the Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant give priority to the timely submission of the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period as required under afore-cited COA rules and regulations to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit thereof and promptly inform the management of the deficiencies noted, if any.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial reports, together with all supporting documents on the prescribed reglementary period required under afore-cited COA rules and regulations to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit thereof and promptly inform the management of the deficiencies noted, if any.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Chairman, thru the BAC Secretariat or designated personnel to: ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts/purchase orders together with each of all the documents forming parts thereof within five (5) working days, from execution/issuance, to warrant timely review and evaluation of the same and enable the audit team to promptly inform the management of the deficiencies, if any; ensure that all entries in the Purchase Orders should be completely and properly filled up; and religiousiously adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 16, 1996.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the BAC Chairman and Secretariat to comply with the audit recommendations.
